commands.name: sign
commands.description: Sign a new app protocol version.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --extra-file
options.description: Bencodex file to use for extra data.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.

options.name: --extra
options.description: Set a value to a key on extra Bencodex dictionary.  Can be applied multiple times (e.g., `-e foo=1 -e bar=baz').  This option implies the extra data to be a Bencodex dictionary, hence cannot be used together with -E/--extra-file option.

arguments.name: 0: KEY-ID
arguments.description: A private key to use for signing. (Required)

arguments.name: 1: VERSION
arguments.description: A version number to sign. (Required)

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify a given app protocol version token's signature.

options.name: --public-keys
options.description: Public key(s) to be used for verification.  Can be applied multiple times.

options.name: --no-key-store
options.description: Do not use any keys in the key store, but only -p/--public-key options.

arguments.name: 0: APV-TOKEN
arguments.description: An app protocol version token to verify.  Read from the standard input if omitted.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Parse and analyze a given app protocol version token.

options.name: --json
options.description: Print information of given token as JSON.

arguments.name: 0: APV-TOKEN
arguments.description: An app protocol version token to analyze.  Read from the standard input if omitted.

commands.name: query
commands.description: Query app protocol version (a.k.a. APV) of target node.

arguments.name: 0: TARGET
arguments.description: Comma separated peer information({pubkey},{host},{port}) of target node.  (E.g., 027bd36895d68681290e570692ad3736750ceaab37be402442ffb203967f98f7b6,9c.planetarium.dev,31236) (Required)

options.name: --path
options.description: Specify key store path to list.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new private key.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --json
options.description: Print created private key as Web3 Secret Storage format.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Do not add to the key store, but only show the created key.

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove a private key.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

arguments.name: 0: KEY-ID
arguments.description: A key UUID to remove. (Required)

commands.name: import
commands.description: Import a raw private key or Web3 Secret Storage.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Do not add to the key store, but only show the created key.

arguments.name: 0: PRIVATE-KEY
arguments.description: A raw private key in hexadecimal string, or path to Web3 Secret Storage to import (Required)

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export a raw private key (or public key).

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --public-key
options.description: Export a public key instead of private key.

options.name: --bytes
options.description: Print raw bytes instead of hexadecimal.  No trailing LF appended.

options.name: --json
options.description: Export a Web3 Secret Storage Formatted json

options.name: --path
options.description: Path to key store to export from.

arguments.name: 0: KEY-ID
arguments.description: A key UUID to export. (Required)

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate a raw private key without storing it.

options.name: --no-address
options.description: Do not show a derived address.

options.name: --public-key
options.description: Show a public key as well.

commands.name: sign
commands.description: Sign a message.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --binary-output
options.description: A path of the file to save the signature. If you pass '-' dash character, it will print to stdout as raw bytes not hexadecimal string or else. If this option isn't given, it will print hexadecimal string to stdout as default behaviour.

options.name: --store-path
options.description: Path to key store to use key from.

arguments.name: 0: KEY-ID
arguments.description: A key UUID to sign. (Required)

arguments.name: 1: FILE
arguments.description: A path of the file to sign. If you pass '-' dash character, it will receive the message to sign from stdin. (Required)

commands.name: derive
commands.description: Derive public key and address from private key.

options.name: --public-key
options.description: Derive from a public key instead of a private key.

arguments.name: 0: KEY
arguments.description: A raw private key (or a public key if -P/--public-key is used) to derive the address from. (Required)

commands.name: diff
commands.description: Compare two trees via root hash.

arguments.name: 0: KV-STORE
arguments.description: The alias name registered through `planet mpt add' command or the URI included the type of IKeyValueStore implementation and the path where it was used at. <kv-store-type>://<kv-store-path> (e.g., rocksdb:///path/to/kv-store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: STATE-ROOT-HASH
arguments.description: The state root hash to compare. (Required)

arguments.name: 2: OTHER-KV-STORE
arguments.description: The alias name registered through `planet mpt add' command or the URI included the type of IKeyValueStore implementation and the path where it was used at. <kv-store-type>://<kv-store-path> (e.g., rocksdb:///path/to/kv-store) (Required)

arguments.name: 3: OTHER-STATE-ROOT-HASH
arguments.description: Another state root hash to compare. (Required)

commands.name: export
commands.description: Export all states of the state root hash as JSON.

arguments.name: 0: KV-STORE
arguments.description: The alias name registered through `planet mpt add' command or the URI included the type of IKeyValueStore implementation and the path where it was used at. <kv-store-type>://<kv-store-path> (e.g., rocksdb:///path/to/kv-store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: STATE-ROOT-HASH
arguments.description: The state root hash to compare. (Required)

commands.name: add
commands.description: Register an alias name to refer to a key-value store.

arguments.name: 0: ALIAS
arguments.description: The alias to refer to the fully qualified key-value store URI. (Required)

arguments.name: 1: KV-STORE-URI
arguments.description: <kv-store-type>://<kv-store-path> (e.g., rocksdb:///path/to/kv-store) (Required)

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Deregister an alias to a key-value store.

arguments.name: 0: ALIAS
arguments.description: The alias name to deregister. (Required)

commands.name: list
commands.description: List all aliases stored.

commands.name: query
commands.description: Query a state of the state key at the state root hash.  It will print the state as hexadecimal bytes string into stdout.  If it doesn't exist, it will not print anything.

arguments.name: 0: KV-STORE
arguments.description: The alias name registered through `planet mpt add' command or the URI included the type of IKeyValueStore implementation and the path where it was used at. <kv-store-type>://<kv-store-path> (e.g., rocksdb:///path/to/kv-store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: STATE-ROOT-HASH
arguments.description: The state root hash to compare. (Required)

arguments.name: 2: STATE-KEY
arguments.description: The key of the state to query. (Required)

commands.name: chain-ids
commands.description: List all chain IDs.

options.name: --hash
options.description: Show the hash of the chain tip.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

commands.name: build-index-tx-block
commands.description: Build an index for transaction id and block hash.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: OFFSET
arguments.description: block index (Required)

arguments.name: 2: LIMIT
arguments.description: block index (Required)

commands.name: block-hashes-by-tx-id
commands.description: Query block hashes by transaction id.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: TX-ID
arguments.description: tx id (Required)

commands.name: blocks-by-tx-id
commands.description: Query a list of blocks by transaction id.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: TX-ID
arguments.description: tx id (Required)

commands.name: block-by-index
commands.description: Query a block by index.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: BLOCK-INDEX
arguments.description: block index (Required)

commands.name: block-by-hash
commands.description: Query a block by hash.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: BLOCK-HASH
arguments.description: block hash (Required)

commands.name: tx-by-id
commands.description: Query a transaction by tx id.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: TX-ID
arguments.description: tx id (Required)

commands.name: get-tx-nonce
commands.description: Query a tx nonce for the address for the canonical chain.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: ADDRESS
arguments.description: signer address (Required)

commands.name: set-tx-nonce
commands.description: Mutate a tx nonce for the address for the canonical chain.

arguments.name: 0: STORE
arguments.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

arguments.name: 1: ADDRESS
arguments.description: signer address (Required)

arguments.name: 2: NONCE
arguments.description: nonce (Required)

options.name: --path
options.description: The URI denotes the type and path of concrete class for IStore.<store-type>://<store-path> (e.g., rocksdb+file:///path/to/store) (Required)

options.name: --offset
options.description: Starting index offset; supports negative indexing (Default: 0)

options.name: --limit
options.description: Maximum number of results to return; no limit by default

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze the given signed or unsigned transaction.

options.name: --unsigned
options.description: Analyze an unsigned transaction.  This option is mutually exclusive with -i/--ignore-signature.

options.name: --ignore-signature
options.description: Terminate the program with a zero exit code (success) even if the signature is invalid.  However, it still prints the error message to the standard error, and the exit code is still non-zero if the transaction is invalid in other ways.  This option is mutually exclusive with -u/--unsigned.

arguments.name: 0: FILE
arguments.description: The file path of the transaction to analyze.  If a hyphen (-) is given it reads from the standard input (if you want to read just a file named "-", use "./-" instead). (Required)

commands.name: help
commands.description: Show this help message.

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Analyze the given block.

options.name: --output
options.description: The file path to write the analysis result to.  If a hyphen (-) is given it writes to the standard output (if you want to write just a file named "-", use "./-" instead).  If not given, it writes to the standard output. (Default: -)

arguments.name: 0: FILE
arguments.description: The file path of the block to analyze.  If a hyphen (-) is given it reads from the standard input (if you want to read just a file named "-", use "./-" instead). (Required)

commands.name: generate-genesis
commands.description: Generate a genesis block.

options.name: --passphrase
options.description: Take passphrase through this option instead of prompt.  Mutually exclusive with --passphrase-file option.

options.name: --passphrase-file
options.description: Read passphrase from the specified file instead of taking it through prompt.  Mutually exclusive with -p/--passphrase option.  For standard input, use a hyphen (`-').  For an actual file named a hyphen, prepend `./', i.e., `./-'.  Note that the trailing CR/LF is trimmed.

options.name: --validator-key
options.description: A public key to use for validating. (Required)

options.name: --load-assembly
options.description: The path of the assembly DLL to load.  Can be specified multiple times.  Note that all referenced assemblies of the specified assemblies are also loaded automatically if they are placed in the same directory.  Tip: In case there are missing references, you can use `dotnet publish` to copy all the required assemblies to a single directory. (Default: System.String[])

options.name: --policy-factory
options.description: The qualified name of the factory method to instantiate a IBlockPolicy<T>.  The factory method must be static and has no parameters.  An assembly that the factory method and the policy type belong to has to be loaded using -A/--load-assembly option.

options.name: --format
options.description: Output format. (Default: Bencodex) (Allowed values: Bencodex, Json)

arguments.name: 0: KEY-ID
arguments.description: A private key to use for signing. (Required)

arguments.name: 1: FILE
arguments.description: File to write the genesis block to.  Use `-` for stdout (you can still refer to a file named "-" by "./-"). (Required)
